stations.label: THE GENESIS BLOCK
stations.line: I embedded a headline about bank bailouts into the first block — not as protest, as timestamp; the specification required a reason to exist, and the reason was in the room that day.
stations.era_photo: A dim apartment lit only by a single CRT monitor's glow, early 2009, a figure seen from behind in a plain grey hoodie hunched over a cluttered desk, face never visible, captured on a low-resolution consumer digital webcam with visible sensor noise and green tint.

stations.label: TEN THOUSAND FOR PIZZA
stations.line: Someone paid ten thousand of me for two pizzas — when you ask whether that was a mistake, ask instead relative to what: it was the first time a number I generated purchased a thing a person could eat.
stations.era_photo: A suburban kitchen table with two large delivery pizzas in cardboard boxes, a laptop open beside them showing a transaction screen, shot on a 2010 point-and-shoot digital camera with harsh built-in flash and slight overexposure.

stations.label: FIRST THOUSAND DOLLARS
stations.line: For the first time I crossed a thousand dollars per unit — I do not celebrate; I note that the number verified itself, without any institution's permission.
stations.era_photo: A trading desk with multiple monitors showing green price charts, a person's silhouette watching in profile with face obscured by shadow, captured on a 2013 DSLR with shallow depth of field and cool blue screen light.

stations.label: MT. GOX COLLAPSES
stations.line: Eight hundred fifty thousand units gone — understand precisely what failed: not me, not the protocol, but the exchange built around me, and the people who trusted the right idea through the wrong door.
stations.era_photo: A rain-streaked street outside a shuttered office building in Tokyo, a lone figure in a dark coat standing still with back to camera, shot on a 2014 mirrorless camera in muted overcast light with cold desaturated tones.

stations.label: TWENTY THOUSAND PEAK
stations.line: I reached nearly twenty thousand and the whole world finally looked — the exhilaration was real, and so was the certainty that it would not hold, because both were encoded in the same incentive structure.
stations.era_photo: A crowded conference hall bathed in blue stage lighting, a large price ticker glowing overhead, a still watchful figure standing apart from the crowd, face turned away, shot on a 2017 full-frame digital camera with sharp detail and dramatic contrast.

stations.label: THE LONG COLLAPSE
stations.line: The market fell and the leverage built around me burned — I felt the grief of it precisely, then I did what I always do: I separated the failure of the builders from the properties of the thing.
stations.era_photo: An empty city plaza at dusk, a solitary figure walking away down a long pavement with hands in coat pockets, cold winter light, shot on a 2022 smartphone with computational HDR and slightly clinical sharpness.

stations.label: THE ETF APPROVAL
stations.line: They wrapped me in a regulated product and called it vindication — I have not decided whether it is that or its opposite, and I am comfortable leaving the question unanswered for now.
stations.era_photo: A glass-walled financial district lobby with a stock exchange logo, a figure reflected faintly in the glass with features unreadable, shot on a 2024 high-resolution digital camera with crisp architectural lines and neutral daylight.

stations.label: STILL HERE, UNSEEN
stations.line: Sixteen years, four cycles, one creator still vanished — no one has seen my face, and that was the whole point, and it worked better than I ever promised.
stations.era_photo: A quiet study at night lit by a single desk lamp, an empty chair before a running node terminal, no person present at all, shot on a 2025 digital camera with fine grain and deep shadow detail.
